sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Real Betis are struggling, with no wins in their last 3 matches and a mixed record of draws and losses in their last 5 games. Deportivo Alavés, however, are in better form, having secured 2 wins in their last 5 matches, including a recent 2-1 victory.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters, Alavés have dominated with a 3-1 win and a 0-0 draw, showing their edge over Betis. Historically, Alavés have not lost to Betis in the last 5 head-to-head matches.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Both teams have scored and conceded equally in the early season (1-1), but Alavés sit higher in the league table (6th vs Betis' 10th). Betis show strength in creating big chances (ranked 4th), while Alavés have a slight edge in recent finishing.

sections.subtitle: Match Dynamics
sections.text: Betis struggle with low possession (32%) and finishing, missing key opportunities. Alavés, with better recent results and defensive stability, might exploit Betis’ vulnerabilities, though matches between these sides often see low goals.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half of the match between Real Betis and Deportivo Alavés at Estadio de la Cartuja ended with a narrow 1-0 lead for the home side. Real Betis took the lead through a well-taken goal by Giovani Lo Celso in the 16th minute, capitalizing on their attacking opportunities. Despite the scoreline, both teams showed moments of quality, with Real Betis creating more clear-cut chances, though they failed to convert several big opportunities. Deportivo Alavés, on the other hand, showed resilience, pushing forward with intent but missing crucial chances to level the game. The game remains open, with both sides displaying potential to influence the outcome in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking momentum between the two teams. Initially, Deportivo Alavés started with strong intent, dominating the early minutes with consistent forward pressure. However, Real Betis shifted the tide around the midpoint of the half, taking control with a series of attacking spells that led to their goal. After conceding, Alavés fought back with renewed vigor, generating several dangerous moments and regaining momentum in patches. Towards the end of the half, Real Betis managed to reassert themselves with a late surge, while Alavés struggled to maintain consistent threat in the closing stages. This back-and-forth dynamic suggests both teams are capable of creating chances in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: Giovani Lo Celso has been the standout player for Real Betis, not only scoring the opener but also dictating play with key passes and involvement in dangerous areas. His composure and vision have been pivotal in breaking through Alavés' defense. For Deportivo Alavés, Jon Guridi has posed a significant threat upfront, coming close to scoring on multiple occasions despite missing big chances. Defensively, Real Betis’ backline, particularly Marc Bartra, has been solid with crucial clearances and interceptions, while Alavés’ Jonny Otto has been instrumental with strong tackling to disrupt Betis’ attacks. These individual contributions could shape the second half significantly.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: Real Betis have shown a slight edge in controlling possession and creating high-quality chances, often penetrating the penalty area with intent. Their approach has relied on exploiting spaces in the final third, though their finishing needs improvement after missing key opportunities. Deportivo Alavés have been more direct, frequently entering the final third and winning possession in advanced areas, but their inability to convert big chances has cost them. Defensively, Betis have been organized, while Alavés have shown more physicality in duels, which could play a role in a tighter second half. Adjustments in finishing and defensive compactness will be crucial for both sides.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Areas for improvement
halftimeSections.text: For Real Betis, the primary concern is their inefficiency in front of goal despite creating several promising situations. They need to be more clinical to secure the game, as leaving chances unconverted could invite pressure from Alavés. Additionally, maintaining defensive focus against Alavés’ direct style will be key. For Deportivo Alavés, capitalizing on their created opportunities is paramount, as they’ve shown the ability to break through Betis’ defense but lack the final touch. Tightening up defensively to avoid conceding on the counter will also be critical if they are to mount a comeback.
